originalTitle: South African jazz legend Abdullah Ibrahim dies at 91
title: A Farewell to Our Music Star, Abdullah Ibrahim
content:
Hello, friends! Today we have some news about a very special person from Africa. His name was Abdullah Ibrahim. He was from South Africa. Abdullah Ibrahim was like a superstar of music. He played the piano. And he played it so beautifully. He taught us many things with his amazing music. He lived a very long life. He was 91 years old. That is many, many years! He shared his wonderful music with people all around the world. We will always remember his beautiful songs and the joy he brought.

Who Was Abdullah Ibrahim?

Abdullah Ibrahim was born in South Africa. He loved music from when he was just a little boy. He started playing the piano when he was very young. Can you imagine playing music almost your whole life? He truly made his piano sing. His fingers would dance on the keys. He made music that sounded like his home, South Africa. It had a special beat. It had special feelings that warmed your heart. He was a truly talented and kind man. *Did you know?* The piano has many black and white keys! You press them gently to make different sounds.

What is Jazz Music?

Abdullah Ibrahim was known for playing jazz music. Jazz is a very special kind of music. It can make you feel happy and want to clap your hands. It can make you feel like dancing and singing along. Sometimes, it can make you feel a little thoughtful too. Jazz music often has a fun, swinging rhythm. Many different instruments play together in jazz music. Pianos, trumpets, drums, and guitars are some of them. Abdullah Ibrahim made South African jazz. This was his very own special type of jazz. It sounded different and wonderfully unique.

Playing Music for a Long, Long Time

Abdullah Ibrahim played music for many, many years. The news says he played for "eight decades." That sounds like a very big number, right? Let's break it down! A decade is 10 years. So, eight decades is 8 x 10 = 80 years! Wow, that's a long, long time! Can you imagine doing something you love for 80 whole years? That is almost his entire life! He started playing music when he was a young boy. He kept playing it when he grew up. And he played it when he became an old man too. He loved music so much that he never stopped sharing his special gift with us. *Did you know?* Some people say music is like a special language that everyone in the world can understand, no matter where they are from!

His Music Taught Us About South Africa

Abdullah Ibrahim’s music was extra special because it sounded like his home country, South Africa. He used sounds and rhythms that came from his roots. His music helped tell stories about South Africa. It helped people feel proud of where they came from and who they were. He helped "define a genre." This means he helped create a whole *new kind* of music. It was a very special style of jazz. It was called South African jazz. When you listened to it, you knew right away it came from South Africa. It was truly unique and beautiful. *Did you know?* South Africa is a big, beautiful country at the bottom tip of Africa! It has many different kinds of people, cultures, and amazing music.

Music Can Make a Difference

Long ago, things were not fair in South Africa. Some people were treated badly because of their skin colour. This made many people sad. It made them feel not strong or happy. Abdullah Ibrahim used his music to help. His music was like a strong, kind voice. It sang about hope. It sang about fairness for everyone. It made people feel brave and helped them stand up for themselves. His songs were like warm hugs for people's hearts, making them feel stronger. His music travelled all over the world. It told everyone about the wonderful people of South Africa. It showed the world how special and strong South Africa is. Music can be very powerful!

Sharing Music with the World

Abdullah Ibrahim didn't just play music in South Africa. He travelled to many, many countries across the globe! He played his piano for people everywhere, from big cities to small towns. He shared the special sounds of South Africa with the whole wide world. People in faraway places loved his music so much. They loved the special rhythm that made them want to tap their feet. They loved the beautiful piano sounds that filled the air. He was like a music ambassador for South Africa. He taught people about his country through his amazing songs. He made many friends around the world with his incredible talent. *Did you know?* An ambassador is someone who represents their country in another country! Abdullah Ibrahim was like a music ambassador, but with his piano!

A Musical Composer

Abdullah Ibrahim was also a very talented composer. This means he wrote his own music. He didn't just play songs that other people wrote. He made up his own melodies. He created his own rhythms and beats. He put his own special feelings into every piece of music. Imagine making up your very own song all by yourself! That is what a composer does. He wrote many, many beautiful songs. These songs are still played today by other musicians. They will be played for a very, very long time. His music will live on forever in our hearts. *Did you know?* Some composers write music for big orchestras, with lots of different instruments like violins, flutes, and drums all playing together!

Saying Goodbye to a Legend

Now, Abdullah Ibrahim has passed away. He lived a truly wonderful and full life. He shared so much beauty and joy with all of us through his music. A "legend" is someone very famous and important. It is someone who did something amazing that people will always remember. Abdullah Ibrahim was truly a music legend. He leaves behind many, many beautiful songs for us to enjoy. He leaves behind many happy memories for those who knew him. We will remember his kind smile. We will remember his bright, warm eyes. But most of all, we will remember his incredible music that touched so many souls. *Did you know?* When someone passes away, we can remember them by listening to their music, looking at their pictures, or telling their stories to others!

What Abdullah Ibrahim Taught Us

Abdullah Ibrahim taught us that music is powerful. It can make us feel happy when we are sad. It can make us feel strong when we are scared. It can even help bring people together and change the world for the better. He taught us to share our special gifts with others. He taught us to be proud of our home and our culture. He showed us that one person, with just a piano, can touch many, many hearts. Let's listen to his music and keep his memory alive by sharing his songs. Thank you, Abdullah Ibrahim, for all the beautiful music and the wonderful lessons you left us.
summary: Abdullah Ibrahim, a much-loved music star from South Africa who played beautiful jazz piano for many years, has passed away at 91, leaving behind a legacy of wonderful songs.
whyMatters: This is important because Abdullah Ibrahim made special music that taught the world about South Africa, helped people feel hopeful, and his beautiful songs will make us happy for a very long time.

originalTitle: MSF staff abused Sudanese refugees in sex-for-food scandal
title: Helping Hands Must Always Be Kind
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, super friends! Your friendly news reporter from Africa is here. Today we are going to talk about something very important: kindness and helping each other. We will talk about people who help others, and what happens when someone is not kind. Remember, it's always good to be kind and to tell a trusted grown-up if you see something that isn't right.

What Are Refugees?

Imagine your home. It's safe and warm, and you play with your friends. Now imagine if you had to leave your home very quickly. Maybe there was a big storm, or maybe there was fighting. You would need to find a new, safe place. This is what happens to people we call refugees.

Refugees are grown-ups and children who have to leave their homes because it's not safe anymore. They might travel a long way to find a place where they can be safe. They need a lot of help when they get to a new place.

Did you know? Many countries in Africa help refugees from other places. It's like welcoming new friends to your neighbourhood when they need a place to stay.

Wonderful Helpers: Doctors Without Borders

When people become refugees, they need many things. They need food to eat, clean water to drink, warm blankets, and doctors if they get sick. There are many kind groups of people who help them. One very famous group is called MSF. That stands for Doctors Without Borders. It's a big name, but it just means doctors and nurses who travel to places all over the world to help people who need it most.

Doctors Without Borders are heroes! They bring medicine, they help babies, and they make sure everyone has what they need to stay healthy. They work in places called refugee camps. A refugee camp is like a temporary town where refugees live together while they wait to find new homes.

Did you know? Doctors Without Borders helps people in almost 90 countries around the world! That's a lot of helping hands!

How Do Helpers Help?

In refugee camps, Doctors Without Borders and other helpers give people many important things. They give out food, so no one goes hungry. They make sure there is clean water to drink. They also give blankets to keep people warm and safe places to sleep. They even build small schools so children can keep learning and playing.

Imagine getting a warm meal and a clean blanket after a long journey. That's the wonderful help these good people give. They are supposed to make sure everyone feels safe and cared for.

Did you know? Food is one of the most important things for our bodies. It gives us energy to play and learn. So, getting enough food is super important!

A Sad Story: When Helpers Were Not Kind

Now, sometimes, even in places where there are many good helpers, a sad thing can happen. We heard a story from a place where refugees were getting help. Some grown-ups who worked for the helpers did something very wrong. Instead of just giving the food and help, they asked for other things that were not kind or fair.

This made some of the refugee grown-ups and children feel very sad and scared. Everyone who gets help should be treated with kindness and respect. No one should ever ask for something extra or unfair when they are giving help.

These grown-ups were supposed to be helping, but they did not follow the rules of kindness. This is a very serious problem.

Feeling Scared to Speak Up

When someone is unkind or asks for unfair things, it can be scary to tell someone. The grown-up refugees in this story felt very scared. They thought if they told someone about the unkind grown-ups, they might not get any more food or help. They were worried that the helpers would stop caring for them.

Imagine being hungry and thinking if you tell someone about bad things, you won't get food anymore. That's a really hard feeling. This is why it's so important for all helpers to be good and trustworthy.

Did you know? It's never your fault if someone is unkind to you. And it's always brave to tell a trusted grown-up, even if it feels scary.

Always Tell a Trusted Grown-Up!

Even though it was scary, some brave people did tell their story. This is so important! If someone is unkind to you, or makes you feel unsafe, or asks for something unfair, you must tell a trusted grown-up. This could be your mom, dad, a teacher, an auntie, or anyone you trust very much.

When you tell, it helps grown-ups know about the problem. It's like telling your teacher if someone is being a bully. The teacher can then help make things fair and safe again. Telling someone is the first step to making things right.

Making Things Right Again

When the leaders of Doctors Without Borders heard about these unkind grown-ups, they were very sad and very serious about fixing it. They said this is not okay. They immediately started to look into what happened. They want to find out exactly who did these wrong things.

They are working hard to make sure that those unkind grown-ups cannot work as helpers anymore. They want to make sure that every helper is kind and fair. This way, people can get help without feeling scared or worried. It is very important to make sure that help is given with kindness and respect, always.

Did you know? Many big organizations like Doctors Without Borders have special rules to make sure all their helpers are kind and fair. If someone breaks these rules, they get into big trouble.

Most Helpers Are Wonderful!

It's important to remember that most grown-ups who work for Doctors Without Borders and other groups are truly wonderful people. They want to help and make the world a better place. They work very hard to bring smiles and comfort to people who need it most.

This sad story about a few unkind grown-ups does not mean that all helpers are bad. It just means we all need to make sure we watch out for each other and speak up when something is not right. We all deserve kindness.

Let's Be Kind and Safe!

So, my super friends, what did we learn today? We learned that refugees are people who need help and kindness. We learned that groups like Doctors Without Borders are amazing for giving help. And we learned that if someone is unkind or asks for unfair things, it's super important to tell a trusted grown-up.

Being kind is one of the best things you can do. Always be kind to others, and always remember that you deserve kindness too. If you ever feel unsafe, or if someone makes you feel uncomfortable, find a grown-up you trust and tell them. They will help you make things right.

Let’s all make sure our world is full of kindness and safety for everyone!
summary: Some helpers who were supposed to give food and other aid to refugees in Sudan were unkind and asked for unfair things, making people scared, but now grown-ups are working to fix this and make sure everyone gets kind help.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone deserves kindness and respect, especially when they need help, and it teaches us that we should always speak up to a trusted grown-up if someone is unkind or makes us feel unsafe.

originalTitle: Norwegian crown princess's son found guilty of two counts of rape
title: Norwegian crown princess's son found guilty of two counts of rape
content: Marius Borg Høiby, the 29-year-old son of Norway's Crown Princess Mette Marit, has been sentenced to four years in jail.

originalTitle: VAR official says hand gesture was 'involuntary, subconscious twitch'
title: VAR official says hand gesture was 'involuntary, subconscious twitch'
content: Video assistant referee Shaun Evans has denied "intentionally" making a hand gesture "to communicate a message, affiliation, game or belief of any kind" and claimed it was a "an involuntary, subconscious twitch" that he was "unaware" of.

originalTitle: The 40-year-old keeper who inspired Cape Verde's historic debut against Spain
title: Cape Verde's Amazing Football Hero Makes History!
content:
Hello, Super Sports Fans!

Hi everyone! Your friendly news reporter from Africa here! Today, I have a super exciting story about football, heroes, and big dreams coming true. Get ready to cheer because something amazing happened in the world of football, and it’s all thanks to a brave team and a very special goalkeeper! This story will make you smile!

What Happened? A Big Surprise!

Imagine a game of football. You know, that fun game where two teams try to kick a ball into a net? Well, there was a very important football game. A team from a small African country called Cape Verde played against a very, very famous team from Spain. Most people thought Spain would win easily because they are known for being super strong at football. But guess what? Cape Verde did not let them win! The game ended in a draw, which means both teams scored zero goals. It was 0-0! This was a huge surprise and made everyone super happy for Cape Verde!

Meet Our Hero Goalkeeper!

Every team has a goalkeeper, right? That’s the player who stands in front of the net and tries to stop the other team from scoring goals. In this game, Cape Verde had an amazing goalkeeper. He is 40 years old! That might sound a little old for someone running around and jumping in a football game, but he was incredible. He jumped, he dived, he caught the ball, and he stopped every single shot from the big Spanish team. He played like a superhero! Did you know? The job of a goalkeeper is one of the most important jobs in football! They are the last line of defence for their team.

What is Cape Verde?

Cape Verde is a beautiful country. It's a group of islands off the coast of West Africa. It's not a very big country, but it has a very big heart! Their football team might not be as famous as teams from bigger countries, but they showed everyone how strong and determined they are. This game was a very special one for them, a real chance to show their skills to the world. Did you know? Cape Verde is famous for its beautiful beaches and vibrant music!

Who is Spain?

Spain is a big country in Europe. Their football team is one of the best in the whole world! They have won many big trophies and have many famous players. So, for a small team like Cape Verde to play against them and not lose, it's like a tiny mouse playing against a big lion and the lion can't catch the mouse! It shows how much courage Cape Verde had. They were brave and strong. Did you know? Spain is famous for delicious food like paella and flamenco dancing!

A "Goalless Draw" – What Does That Mean?

When we say the game was a "goalless draw," it means that neither team managed to score a goal. The final score was 0-0. Both teams tried their best to kick the ball into the net, but no one could. For Cape Verde, this 0-0 score was like a big win! It meant they held their ground against a much stronger team. The amazing goalkeeper made sure no ball got past him. He was like a wall!

A "Historic Debut" – Wow!

This game was also a "historic debut" for Cape Verde. "Debut" means the very first time something happens. So, it was the first time Cape Verde played a big, famous team like Spain. And "historic" means it was so important that people will remember it for a very, very long time. It’s a moment that will be written in the history books of Cape Verdean football! They showed the whole world what they can do. Did you know? A team's first big game against a strong opponent is often called their debut match!

Our Goalkeeper Became "Viral"!

When something goes "viral" on social media, it means lots and lots of people see it very, very quickly! Everyone on their phones and computers was talking about Cape Verde's amazing goalkeeper. People were sharing videos of his incredible saves. He became famous overnight because he played so, so well! It was like everyone around the world was cheering for him. He showed that age is just a number if you have skill and passion.

Never Give Up – A Big Lesson!

This story teaches us something very important. It teaches us to never give up, even if you are small or people don't expect you to win. Cape Verde was the "underdog" – that's what we call the team that people think won't win. But they didn't listen to what others thought. They played with all their hearts. The goalkeeper, being 40 years old, showed that you can still be brilliant and strong, no matter your age. He inspired his whole team to play better. Did you know? Many amazing athletes play their best even when they are older, showing that experience can be very powerful!

Teamwork Makes the Dream Work!

While the goalkeeper was a super hero, the whole Cape Verde team played brilliantly too. They all worked together, running, passing, and defending. Teamwork is like when you and your friends work together to build a sandcastle or solve a puzzle. When everyone helps each other, amazing things can happen. This team showed wonderful teamwork and made their country very, very proud.

Dreams Can Come True!

For the people of Cape Verde, this game was more than just football. It was a dream come true. It showed that with hard work, belief, and a little bit of magic (from their amazing goalkeeper!), anything is possible. It makes all of Africa proud to see a team from our continent do so well on the big stage. It's a reminder that even the smallest places can have the biggest heroes. Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in the world, played and watched by billions of people!

A Story to Remember!

So, next time you play football or any game, remember the amazing team from Cape Verde and their 40-year-old goalkeeper. Remember how they stood tall against a giant and made history. It’s a story about courage, passion, and the power of dreams. What an inspiration! We can all learn from their amazing spirit. Keep chasing your dreams, just like Cape Verde chased their football dream!
summary: A 40-year-old goalkeeper from the small African country of Cape Verde became a hero by helping his team draw 0-0 against the big football team from Spain, making history and inspiring everyone around the world.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows that even small teams can do great things with courage and teamwork, proving that age is just a number and that dreams can come true if you work hard and believe in yourself.

title: 'Cartwheels at breakfast' & kicking about Boston - the culture shift in Scotland camp
content: Steve Clarke "doing cartwheels" is a sign of the shift among the Scotland squad and staff, writes Amy Canavan in Charlotte.
